work.note: Following the Grammarly acquisition. Leading accessibility across the Superhuman surface: keyboard-first interaction model, screen-reader coverage, a cross-functional a11y council of 12. Shipping an annual external accessibility report.
highlights: Moved the flagship inbox from partial WCAG 2.1 AA to verified 2.2 AA across desktop and web
Hired and grew a dedicated a11y engineering partner
Published the "Accessible by default" internal playbook — now mandatory reading for new PMs

work.note: Joined as a staff product designer on the writing assistant. Moved into an accessibility-lead role in 2022, partnering with engineering and research across five product surfaces.
highlights: Redesigned the suggestion card interaction for non-visual use — reduced average tab cost from 9 to 3
Ran a 14-person cross-functional council; shipped tokenised motion & contrast primitives
Coached six ICs into senior roles

work.note: Platform design — plugins, the component system, and the library model. Quietly began the company's first accessibility working group.
highlights: Shipped the plugin-permissions model, still in use today
Founded an internal a11y working group with five volunteers

work.note: Two-person shop. Product design and frontend engineering for early-stage teams. Shipped eight products; half are still running.
highlights: Designed & built the initial web app for a healthtech startup now serving 2M users
Wrote a small design-system library that went on to have a surprisingly long tail on GitHub

work.note: My first real job. Long-form interactive storytelling. Learned to ship on deadlines I did not set, for audiences that did not know I existed.
highlights: Built 30+ interactive features; two were nominated for a Malofiej
First introduction to accessibility, through a project with a blind sports correspondent

work.note: Thesis on non-visual navigation of complex documents. Where I fell in love with the work I still do.
